strengths: Exceeds the 15-year experience minimum with ~19 years across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and community leadership through conference talks (FOSS4G 2019) and multi-day workshops/courses
concerns: No recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products/platforms; AI exposure is limited to ~1 year of MCP tooling and agent-infrastructure contributions at re:cinq
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field (Bachelor's in CS only), against the role's preferred qualifications
Career level tops out at Staff/Senior engineer; no evidence of director-level strategic influence, advising strategic customers, or driving org-wide technical direction

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. director-level IC scope

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: ~19 years of engineering experience (2006–present) and a BS in Computer Science. Entrepreneurial experience is concretely evidenced by founding GISCollective, and public-speaking evidence (FOSS4G talk, Selenium and Web Internals workshops) partially supports the 'inspire others' responsibility. However, the role's center of gravity is recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products and cloud/AI strategy at director level, and the résumé shows only recent, tactical AI-adjacent work (MCP code generators since April 2025), no advanced AI degree, and no evidence of strategic advisory or org-level technical leadership — the highest title held is Staff Software Engineer. Strong hands-on breadth cannot substitute for the missing AI depth and strategic seniority, so fit is weak despite solid fundamentals.
